pages.sketch_desc: Hand-drawn sketch of a lakeside landscape viewed from the campsite. Shows a lake (Lake Piano or Lake Lugano area) with wavy shoreline on the left, a row of buildings/town in the middle, tent/camping symbols scattered on the right hillside, and what appears to be a mountainous backdrop with hatching. A castle or large walled structure is drawn on the right side. Caption written diagonally reads 'View O'Kamping Rivetta, Porlezza.'
pages.text: View O'Kamping Rivetta, Porlezza.

We turned off to Camping OK __Porlezza__ + swam in Lake Piano, between Lugano + Como.
   __Thursday__ Tried to swim but take locked at 8.15. [?Lucrhetofen] ← [?Section] [illegible]
   We set off + stopped at __Crema__ + saw the Duomo with an enormous façade, much bigger than the cathedral inside. Within, the architecture remind[s] us of English that Norman [?]. Brick, simple, dignified.
   On to __Soncino__, (the driving has been tiring, around __Lake Como__,) nowhere to stop for elevenses, a quick glance at the Villa d'Este Hotel. Simon got so cross at the ferry office that we drove instead of taking the ferry.
   At Soncino we stopped by a lovely castle + moat + the boys played football with a group of

local boys + girls. Friendlier people than in the morning.
     We spent the night at the __Cremona__ campsite, + had pizzas in a friendly restaurant where we

watched them putting pizzas in the oven. We got lost on the way out of the city centre back to the campsite.
     At every campsite John + Charles spend ages in the children's playground. At Cremona William + Peter played ping pong.
__Friday__  We set off around __Cremona__, looking at the Targetti lighting shop, the Stradivarius museum & the town hall which had a collection of lovely old violins.
     We had lunch in a self-service restaurant + couldn't get into the cathedral. Simon + the boys spent ages deciding which violin models to buy.

captions: ANDREA AMATI IL "CARLO IX DI FRANCIA" 1566
GIUSEPPE GUARNERI figlio di Andrea IL "QUARESTANI" 1689
GIUSEPPE GUARNERI detto del Gesu LO "STAUFFER" 1734

pages.text:
We left & passed through a spa town, __Salso[?Maggiore]__ Terme, & found for signs to a campsite out in the country. It is big & could be dreadful but the people are friendly & we are very fortunate to be camped right on the edge with a marvellous view. __Arizona__***** With all mod cons here, we are looking out across a scene from a mediaeval book of hours. I can see little fields from here - at least 15 of them, all of different colours & textu[res] & woodland & solitary trees, old farm houses & barns, beautiful tractored rows of hay drying in the sun. The house across the way, the children have remarked, looks like the first illustration in "James & the Giant Peach".
  The camp itself has 3 swimming pools, 2 shoots, a throbbing disco (until 3am) a laundrette & a very good restaurant where

captions: Barn across from Arizona swimming pool
pages.text:
Barn across from Arizona swimming pool

[continuing from previous page] had Sunday lunch - lasagna & roast veal + pote - for £40. I've done 2 loads of wash at vast expense £4, much to Simon's horror. Blissfully hot, but bearable with lots of trees.
	The man next to us on crutches has a very amusing green parrot called Loretto. His wife bops it when it screeds too much. She has great trouble getting

captions: House across from camping van at Arizona.
pages.text:
House across from camping van at Arizona.

it into a cage at night time.
	On Saturday evening at 6:15. an old Franciscan came round ringing a handbell "la Santa Messa alle sette". They set up an alter in the ping pong shed. The children came & went, a lovely service. I could watch the boys on the slide & swings behind the altar.
	We have spent our time eating & swimming. John & William did a [illegible] of

the playground.
		We've had a family ping pong tournament. Simon & I beat everyone, but he beat me.
	__Monday July 19th.__
		We left & went to __Parma__. What with one thing (Peter needing to pee) & another (everyone window shopping) we got to the Duomo just as it shut. We had difficulty finding a lunch place. The boys behaved dreadfully in the pink marble baptistery & cathedral & Eve got fed up & dehydrated. We then saw St Giovanni Evangelista & went back to the van. We camped at __Parma__, where there was an open air concert with a blond singer, but we couldn't go because of the children
	__July 20th.__
		*** The campsite has a resident fun fair. John & Charles & Peter went on a merry-go-round yesterday & all the boys

trampolined on a 10 bouncer.
		Today we took John & Charles again.

We had trouble finding __Torrechiara__. It is marvellous, + I frightened the boys into better behaviour. Gorgeous Romanesque arches + pleasing proportions in the open courtyard. Probably built c 1460. Frescoes in most of the rooms. Dry, sunny.
    Afterwards we had an expensive lunch down in the village in a restaurant of the hotel, which would be nice to stay in. There was only one other group of people visiting the castle - the man had great trouble convincing the ticket-seller that none of our children belonged to him. Baghoni + Benedetto were the fresco painters. The Camera d'Oro was beautiful.
    We drove on over hilly roads - beautiful scenery over Sillara pass 60% height St. Gottard. A cyclist at the top stopped to put brown paper inside his shirt, to stop his sweat

Entrance ticket to __Torrechiara__

making him cold, before whizzing to the bottom.
    At the far side we came down to __Berceto__. A lovely town with a beautiful cathedral - plain Romanesque. Tympanum outside about 1200? Crucifixion or later. Inside calm, massive, alive. John lit a candle lovely arches + crisp capitals. One of the nicest churches that I've seen. We all had ice cream. Huggledy piggledy roads
    On towards Sestri Levante. We've

stopped to camp in __Borgo val de Taro__.
Campsite in new town, we walk up the hill to the old town. John + Charles play cars with Allesandro.
	__Wednesday July 21st.__
	__Back to handwashing.__
	We went up to shop. Fruit + veg weren't very good - we were 1300 feet up. The boys bought toys. We bought £30 of Parmesan + prosciutto to take home to England.
	The campsite people are very friendly, offering us tables + chairs + umbrellas. At lunch time it rained + thundered. It rained all through the nap. We went up to town again at 3 or 4 o shopped, + were told that the swimming pool was shut because of the rain. It thundered again at supper with bright lightening. Only William was brave enough to sleep in the tent.
	__Thursday July 22nd__
	We packed up. Simon scared a rat away as he folded up the groundsheet. We went up over a pass + Eve felt sick, then on down to __Varese__, a very early example of town planning. We walked around lovely colours in the house painting. On to lunch at 2.30. next to the road.

[Photo of Varese, aerial/elevated view showing town planning with terraced hillside streets and colourful buildings]

Varese, early town planning.
bridge 1519.

[Additional diary text visible around and behind the photo but largely obscured; fragments on left side of image are illegible due to photo overlap]

In the afternoon we tried to camp at Livorno but there was no room. We finally found a camp at __Sestri Levante__ #**Trigoso only 300m. from a station one down from Sestri Levante, which is just what we wanted. Thomas had a headache most of the day + ended up being sick just before bed.
  We traipsed down the hill ½ mile to a restaurant, very friendly, who suggested mussels + Sarsa de noxe - Salse dei noci, ravioli with pine nuts + basil - delicious.
  William has read that Americans eat the most icecreams, Brits eat the second most + Italians the 3rd most, but the Italians spend more money on them. Orson Wells has commented that the Italians are a nation of actors … all x millions of them act, but only the bad ones get into films.
Friday July 23rd.
  We hurried + caught the 9.20. train to Corniglia, to walk back to __Vernazza__. We packed in the Knapsack + bum bag :- suncream, 7 cups, 3 bottles water, bog roll, plastic bag, extra bags +

rubber band to keep John's arm dry, spare shorts, 7 swim suits, one towel, shade sheet, money, passports, biscuits, band aids, maps, sun hats.
  The train journey was 25 minutes. We then walked up a huge flight of steps + drank 2 cups of water each at the village tap. It was a long hot walk. After the village, a sign said that the walk would take 1½ hrs. I guessed it wd. be 2 hours. Infact it took 1¾ hours. A few times there was a sheer drop off the path down a cliff, but most of the walk was beautiful + safe through olive

groves, bamboo + vineyards.

    At Vernazza we had lunch overlooking an inlet of waves which had cut through a tunnel in the rock. John & Charles were fascinated. We had spaghetti at pesto + icecream or crème caramel. Afterwards we swam in the harbour + caught a train home. It is very convenient to be able to walk back to the van from the Station.

    We had a delicious plum latticed tart for supper. But it was very expensive. Veg + fruit are not easy to get here. The campsite is ugly but friendly. We are not quite high enough up to get a good view of the sunsets.

__Saturday July 24th__

    We rushed to wash clothes + wash up to catch the 10.00 train into Sestri Levante. But at the Station Simon was furious that the train fare was more for a 5 min. journey into SL than it was for a 25 mins to Corniglia, so we didn't go by train. Simon + Thomas found a bus for us to take instead.
    We window shopped + saw lovely jewelry + the boys bought yet more cars. Later in the day Eve bought spaghetti tongs. We sat on a bench + on the pavement to eat delicious pizza which Simon bought.
    After a lovely 3 hour swim we shopped for

food on the way back to the campsite.
    Simon cooked supper - tomatoes, lettuce, ham, bread, olives marinated in dill or cumin?, bread, followed by grapes, plums + chestnut profiteroles.
    Supper is being washed up by Peter + dried by John. William + Thomas will clear up after the next meal.

    We showed the boys my favourite hotel, the Villa Balbi 0185-42.941 (fax 0185-482451)

    At the cake shop Charles screamed + I dragged him across the road. The shopowner asked Simon whether he was the boys' father or big brother.
    Simon + I found the campsite playground on a terrace far down the hillside.

__Sunday July 25th__

    We left in a hurry. Other campers were blocking us in with more caravans, + waiting to put up an awning. Simon bumped + cracked a car light.

    We got onto the autostrada + came off at Rapallo to look for a church. Beautiful expensive towered villas, but no church for miles + miles. Finally we found a gilded St Lawrence, where John misbehaved + we left pretty quick.
    A car [illegible] bumped us as we stopped at a pedestrian crossing - her mini light broke. We were on the Via Aurelia, as was our campsite. It was built from Rome around the coast 1830

We had lunch in a layby + drove on to __Cuneo__, where we had trouble following signs to the campsite**. The boys played ping pong. Some of the loos were incredibly old + corrugated + smelled of pigs.
	We walked around a flea market in Cuneo + had supper in a friendly restaurant overcooked spaghetti, good veal + chips. Trattore Belvedere.
	__Monday July 26th__
	We played ping pong + set off towards __les Deux Alps__. We stopped to shop at __Saluzzo__. We went along very windy roads. Near the end of the day we passed a glacier, + finally reached __les Deux Alys__[?], where the 3 boys had skied 2 years before. It was dark when we arrived. Everyone put on trousers, except Eve who didn't have any. Cold, cold. We had supper in the van + walked up the hill for crêpes before bed - lemon, chocolate, honey, chestnut, strawberry.
	__Tuesday July 27th__
	Once the sun came over the hill, the

air warmed up very quickly, + we were able to eat breakfast outdoors. Wild flowers. Friendly campsite man who said that caravans were better than tents in freezing conditions. Van waterworks don't [illegible] hold up when it's freezing.
	We walked around the ski village. William lost a lens + had a headache. The rest of us went up in a ski lift. Les Deux Alps is a village, with "the Village" a hotel complex above it. It's a super place for a summer holiday - mountain biking, scootering downhill after being hauled up on a hand lift, bowling, sliding, paragliding, glacier skiing, walking, horseriding. But Simon found the site rather ugly.
	Charles + John found the chair lift exciting. Charles, Peter + I went in one. Simon, John + Thomas in the other.
	At 12.15. we left + passed __Grenoble__ + ploughed through __Lyons__ in the rush hour. At the last minute we visited the __Fracastos__ + swam, + they gave us a delicious barbequed supper. Afterwards

we drove off & reached __St. Jean de Vaux__ at 10.30. w. of Chalon sur Saone. A gorgeous vingard runs down right to the edge of the campsite. rocky outcrop above! Famous wine names all around. Swimming pool Semion stopped. We washed clothes & swam
Wed.   At 5.30 we went for a walk for an hour through the Burgundy vinyards. It was very hot & quiet, except for John who cried a[?lot] of the way that he wanted a drink of water.
   At 7.30 we left for supper at "Hotel de Raisin" __Givry__. It's quite a large, pretty town, buildings of tired yellow. An imposing building in the middle with a red & yellow patterned, tiled a roof, cf Beaune. The church & other buildings seem C18. (II) windows. Did wealth arrive then, instead of the Middle Ages.
   They are pruning some of the vines. Some fields have feathery rows, others neat.
   Charles can make extraordinary motercycle noises after Italy, to suit all sorts & types & riding conditions of motorbikes.
   We had a delicious meal – one of the best. The woman was very kind helping

Charles so that he didn't burn himself. John practised his "Au revoir, Madame" at the end & was invited to kiss her goodbye, which was quite a shock, but he managed it.
   We had snails, chicken, frogs legs, caramel custard, cheese icecream.
__Thu 29th July__
   John & Charles & I walked into the village to get a stamp & post cards.
   Simon & Thomas found a wine tasting place & we all walked off to Marc Laborde & tasted & bought a dozen of white & red. Staggered back with Simon carrying 1 dozen & 1st Wm carrying the other case ⅓ mile. Eve hung onto John & Charles.
   We made it back for a lovely cool swim. The pool is less crowded in the morning.
   We had lunch & a nap. Then Thomas & Eve went out round the [?ex] village to sit in the shade & sketch. William & Peter swam.
   John had an icecream because he isn't able to swim.
   There are 3 groups of children sleeping in

[Page dominated by two pen-and-ink architectural sketches. Background diary text in faint pencil/light ink is largely illegible beneath the drawings.]

Well + bench.

entrance to courtyard.

[Background text fragments around margins and behind sketches — illegible]

[Page dominated by two pen-and-ink architectural sketches and a place-name label. Background diary text in faint pencil/light ink is largely illegible beneath the drawings.]

Window at side of church.

stone hinge barn door

__St. Jean de Vaux__

[Background text fragments around margins and behind sketches — illegible]

tents in diferent parts of the campsite. They sing while washing up. And a play school of small children arrives with helpers to spend each day here. In one corner of the site there is a sink + French loo set apart for them.

View from our
    campsite

St. J. l. V. vinyards.

captions: Village house R. J. l. V.
pages.text:
[illegible faint text throughout, largely obscured by drawings]

Village house R. J. l. V.

We went to Restaurant du Centre for a meal in the evening, just around the corner. It was delicious. As usual, we ordered 6 meals, with an extra plate for J + C to share. The food was delicious, but dreadfully slow. The first course took 50 minutes to reach the table (eggs + wine [?lees]) the second course 40 minutes (duck/rabbit) + overcooked veg, + the pud. 30 minutes - (sorbet, floating island + crème caramel) with frequent trips to the loo to wipe bottoms in between. Alto gether, it wasn't nearly as enjoyable as the previous evening.

__Friday 30th July__

    Packed up amid much bickering + drove to an E.Leclerk supermarket.
    Set off on a long drive up towards Calais 12.00. Drove + drove, played 'Spot It.'
    We reached Lumbres [?***] at 9. pm. John + Charles got filthy in the play ground. We left early + didn't see the swimming pool, but the river looked pretty.

captions: Watertower / Outside E.Leclerk / near Chalons s. Saone
pages.text:
Watertower
Outside E.Leclerk
near Chalons s. Saone

Sat. 31st July
	We left at 9.30, & had lunch on the Dover-Calais ferry on the way back to Westfield Rd..

__Sat 7th August__
	We set off at nine to take the boys to Rhiwiau Riding Centre. We had lunch on the sea front at [?Llandudas] [?Llanfairfechan]; where Simon tried to sail his boat in a boating pond. After leaving Thomas, William & Peter we took John & Charles swimming
	We walked around __Conwy__ on the walls & saw an aquarium with lobsters & eels & went inside the smallest house in [?Brat] Britain.
	We camped in the middle of nowhere, near a dam. * * *
	In the [?morning] night 2 cars came & picked up someone, perhaps, after flashing lights. We were scared (at least Eve was) & Simon locked the doors.
	It didn't rain the next day, & John & Charles walked 3½ miles up into the hills

After lunch we headed home. We narrowly missed being stuck in a huge traffic jam. When Simon saw that the cars were closing up ahead, he pulled off down side roads for tea. After travelling 3 miles we stopped to put the kettle on & saw the accident over the hedge — the police, 2 ambulances & 2 fire engines. Fortunately, we got home in one piece.

__Friday 13th Aug__
	Left at 6.10. Heavy traffic. Camped in a small, sheltered site, green, with a pond at one end. * * *
John & Charles very good. Supper at 9.pm.
Sat Picked up boys from riding, drove home listening to all their stories. Home by 2pm for lunch. On the day, we looked at "Four Winds", Grandma's & Grandpa's old house outside Telford — we had stopped for petrol just by their old road.

__Friday August 20th__
     We left at 5.45., Jr Cagain in their pyjamas. After supper in the field, William babysat John & Charles while Simon & Thomas & I went in to hear Peter's quartets at __Beauchamps__. His 'cellist's bridge slipped, but they all did very well. Afterwards we listened to "After Cocoa" music. The Fishpols did stalwart work getting the children to bed.
     __Sat__
     We got up in a leisurely way, & then went to explore the __Forest of Dean__ Heritage centre. Wonderful observation bee hive, where we failed to find the queen, an antery, established since 1968, good adventure playground, & museum of mining & tree management in the forest. 27% oaks.
     Back at __Beauchamp__ we had lunch & listened to the orchestra, which included "My Fair Lady". Home at about 6. pm.

Friday August 2[?]
     We left at 5.0., & inspite of the Bank holiday, arrived at __Beauchamp__ at 6.15. Grandma, Grandpa, Aunt Helen & John joined us & had a drink while we ate supper. We heard Thomas play some Telemann with Rupert Budley. He was also in a sextet. The "guards" were on duty until 4.30 am.
     __Sat__
     While Thomas released the rest of us, went to see the cruck barn at __Ashleworth Court__. The church was pretty too — a squint, beautifully tapestried kneelers, & a rare, painted Edward [VI] rude screen. Flooding from [?Severn].
     John & Charles & Eve had a trying walk over a vast field looking for blackberries.
     In the afternoon we heard a lovely concert. John, Peter, Simon & Eve listened while William babysat Charles — which he said was very hard work with just one child. They played the New World Symphony. Afterwards Eve chatted to the mother of Adams, a boy on the course. She had a ten week baby & had written the biography of Laura Ashley.
     Then we packed up & went home.

Sept 2nd Thu.
	 At 6.15. we left for Exmoor, where Gavn will marry Polly Martin on Saturday. We drove + drove + Eve map read a short cut west of Exford, over Silly Bridge. The road sign said ◄ 6ft 6ins ►. The van is 2 metres wide. By the time we realised, we were in an amizing tunnel lane. After aligning the van twice, with Eve walking ahead to point the glaring headlights exactly to the middle of the bridge walls, Simon managed to get the van through. Amazing.
	 We are staying at __Westermill Farm__
Expensive £11 per night, but very good + has attracted foreigners. Clean loo block, (Dinas £4 + grotty loo block) 3 fields, sheltered valley, tame ducks who come right up to beg for bread, sheep. The river runs along the edge of the site + is damned into a pool which John + Charles love.
	 In the morning the boys swam + paddled + then played cricket. After lunch we

pages.sketch_desc: Pencil/blue ink sketch at the top of the page showing two boys (John and Charles) at a pond or dammed river section. The sketch depicts a low bridge or dam structure with water flowing beneath, and two stick-figure children on or near the structure. A rough landscape/bank is sketched to the right. No directional arrows or route labels — this is an illustrative scene sketch, not a route map.
captions: John + Charles at the pond at Westermill Farm
pages.text:
John + Charles at the
pond at Westermill Farm

intended to go on a walk – the campsite publish 4 guides from 1 hour to 2½ hours – but it was so hot that we lay on the grass. While Simon played cricket with Thomas, William + Peter, Eve took John + Charles to paddle.
	 The clothes line pole is in constant use from wet socks as the boys slosh in the river.
	 After tea we went on the "red" walk, up + round the hill, past a menhir + a 4 stone setting – common to Exmoor. Just

pages.sketch_desc: Small hand-drawn sketch of what appears to be a farm building or structure, possibly depicting the moorland farm mentioned in the text. No place labels or route arrows visible.
pages.text:
outside the farm was moorland. Thomas got badly shocked by an electric fence. Charles saw a black sheep & said "look, mummy there's a sheep all covered in pooh!"

[sketch of building/farm structure]

The wall are interesting here - stone walls with beech trees growing out of the top of them. #
		It has become cold with the sun going in.
		John was feeding ducks & said "Mummy, why don't they make ducks with fur?"

__Sat 4th Sept__
		In the morning it was sunny again & we explored __Dunster__, but didn't have time to see the Castle. John got his feet wet in the mill race, & we saw a beautiful pack-horse bridge, meadows, hills, paths & a thatched cottage. Several thatched houses.

[above photo, faint text - largely illegible, possible show-through from reverse]

[glued-in photograph: historical/postcard-style sepia image of a street scene, showing stone buildings, a horse-drawn carriage, and what appears to be a village high street]

[below photo, faint handwritten text:] [illegible] [?when] it [?is] a [?Victorian] [illegible]
[?Our] [?favourite] [illegible]
[illegible]

William got lost, so we were almost late meeting Kate Warburton's train. She had to catch a later one, anyway, because hers had been cancelled. We stood in Taunton station carpark to eat lunch.
    We just had time for John + Charles to swim before Kate + Jamie Hamilton dropped off Kate Jessica + picked up Simon + Eve to go to Gawn's + Polly's wedding. The wedding was in Dulverton Church, + the reception at Withypool.
    Kate + Jamie picked up Jessica the Sunday morning + we set off to Taunton Station to leave Kate.
    For lunch we went a little north to Maunsell Lock, Hedging. __Gorgeous__ blackberrying in the sunshine + a stroll along the canal.

Friday 24 Sept.
    We have come away to the Cotswolds for the wkend. Chris Goerdt, the new German au pair has come too. She is sleeping in the Kyam, + Wm + Pt in the van with S+E, J+C. We left at 5.40 + got to Naunton at [?] 7.10. We have camped at the back of a pub, + use the modern pub loos. Dogs bark in the kennels + a mare + foal is in the next field.
    Sat 25th.
    It rained in the night + was foggy in the morning, but the sun started to shine at midday. The campsite __Naunton__ *** is good, except the [?] dogs bark.
    We left about 9.30. to walk to __Power Guiting__, along narrow roads, past horse farms, over foot bridges. We reached the [illegible] village + had trouble finding a cup of coffee. We bought bread baked at the bakers, + the pub finally opened at 11 — they were preparing for a wedding. Peter threatened to pop the balloons on a gate which marked the bride's house.
    We visited the church where they were preparing for Harvest Festival. Old, but Victorian restorations. A nice modern Wheatsheaf [illegible] in a small stained glass window + a Victorian [illegible] hour glass in the door lintel. Vicar friendly.
    We returned along footpaths. Ramblers were breaking a path through a ploughed field.

In the afternoon Charles, William + Peter did homework + Chris wrote letters while Simon + John went for another walk.

[photo glued in: stone village street with flowers and rooftops]

[illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible] [illegible]

The sky was clear & the night cold.
__Sun 26th Sept__

    It was cold & drizzly in the morning. We went to Naunton church which was friendly, [?]&[?] [?vagram] but full of old people. 1662 Communion with sentences, collect & gospell from ASB. Beautifully kept church & good organist. Stone inscription next to the alter to [?]Oscar's son? "barbarously murdered by rebels in 1645". A list of people who came back in safety from Great War.
    We had coffee & walked around the back roads of the village.
    We drove to __Bourton-on-the-Water__ & couldn't find the hotel we stayed in. We all enjoyed the model village. We wandered around the village & had fish & chips with delicious chips. Then an ice cream.

16th April 1994   Charles' birthday

Simon, Eve (Thomas away on French trip at [?Sassete]) William 12, Claire [?Portal] (11) peter 10, John 6, Charles 5, set off at 9 am. to take Peter + Claire to pony camp. A lovely windy, part-sunny day. Elevenses in the lay-by & lunch on the sea front. Children at the play ground, Simon gone to buy a map.

[Drawing of a stone, labelled:] Charles' stone

captions: A still flexible star fish Peter found for John
pages.text:
A still flexible
star fish
Peter found
for John

Llanfairfechan

[Second starfish drawing, no caption]

[Ticket 1 — printed] CONWY VALLEY RAILWAY MUSEUM / BETWS-Y-COED / VISITOR'S TICKET / CHILD / Valid only for day of issue / [serial no.] 7643
[Ticket 2 — printed] CONWY VALLEY RAILWAY MUSEUM / BETWS-Y-COED / MINIATURE RAILWAY / Valid only for one journey / Issued subject to the Company's regs. / [serial no.] 8187
[Postcard caption — printed] THE CONWY VALLEY   RAILWAY, BETWS-Y-COED

Stone farmhouse bottom of valley.   R. Alwen  s.e of Betws-y-Coed.
Brick porch with wooden 'railway front.'   Dolgungles

   We left Peter and Clare at the riding school. We drove back through __Betws-y-Coed__. Touristy shop + a lovely miniature railway which William, John + Charles went on. Very friendly staff. We looked around the museum, it even had the old smell of a ticket office. We saw the old church - lit only by candles, ceiling falling down. (B Old font, [illegible] with 2 carveson each face.
Charles 6.day cake.

east ward over the hills. The first was too boggy. The second was nonexistant! We came down into a beautiful valley + asked a farmer! wife who said yes. R. Alwen *** When I went to pay her £3 in the morning, she said to give a £ each to the 3 boys. + [?photo] Won tok

April 17th It was cold in the night and overcast in the morning, but at least not raining. We drove to __Gwydelldeon__ [?Gwyddel] for church + arrived just intime for the grace. The kind vicar gave us a blessing after the service. The church had a beautiful carved wooden roof + Victorian additions.

   We had elevenses + walked around the ruins of Valle Crucis Cistercian Monastery. Lovely stones. Dormitory clearly laid out. On through Llangollen, lunch by canal viaduct. We walked along + stepped out on the viaduct + Eve felt very sick with vertigo + backed of. The sun came out for a little.

   On to tea at Grandma's to pick up Thomas after his French trip. (Jim + Margaret + Helen picked him up for us at Chorley this morning.) where Charles had another birthday cake

Friday 22nd April.                   ***

   We left at 7:30, and reached __Dderyddellan__ in time for lunch at 12.30, driving past Betwy-y-Coed again. A 6+6 campsite by the castle. A romantic old Norman Keep, no tourists. After lunch we went for an exhausting 6 mile walk. William sat in the van + Thomas, John, Charles, Simon + I sloshed through peaty water in gum boots. It overflowed some boots - yellow tussock jumping, jumping across streams, Simon sometimes carrying Charles. John jumped 3 ditches. We had cakes + water for tea + disappeared into a forest. Simon kept telling Thomas 'find 70 degrees on your compass.' We wiggled through + finally found a forresters track, + came home along the road. lovely views of the castle, camouflaged by the [?hillside]

   We had lunch outside but supper inside. It rained on + off in the afternoon.

   There were lots of DofE orange tent campers who left before we got up.

Saturday 23rd April.
	We woke & drove through beautiful mountain scenery clockwise, coming to Rhinrian from the west. We picked up Clare Portal & Peter & hurried home to greet Matt + Peter who had been to Holland, + John Warburton arriving via a Stratford wedding with Justin Baltles.

Saturday 30th April	__Just Simon + Eve!__
	5.0. We dropped William & Justin at Blue Goat & set off to hear Thomas in concert at __Upton__ music & arts festival. Lovely old small theatre with minstrel's gallery & high stage, dark brown velvet curtains. Peeling green walls. "Tommy Williams celebrated young Upton treble." Thomas played in his trio with harpsichord twice & a solo. Then he had a 26 mile Marathon the next day.
	We spent the night at __The Anchor Pub__, near Welland, + had a drink at the pub.
	May 1st We returned to Upton to go to church. I really like Upton. There's a camping folk festival, cricket, Marina, interesting & normal shops, old & new buildings.

pages.sketch_desc: A simple hand-drawn sketch of a hill silhouette (Malvern Hills view from the van) with small lines suggesting sunrays or trees on the left side. Also a small sketch of a wind turbine beside the Delabole entry.
pages.text:
View from the van during coffee before church
Malvern Hills. It was a friendly church, with an interesting modern baldachino over the central altar.
	We went on down to motorway & camped near Launceston at __Altarnun__ [?] ugly. People arriving at the house with Bibles at 8.0. Windmills turn in the distance.

May 2nd
	Simon spent ages paying at the camp site. The man had been healed of cancer.
	We stopped at __Delabole__ wind farm. Each blade 16 m. long. Each windmill produces enough electricity for 300 homes. Wind power could produce 10% Britain's [?electricity] needs. First private commercial wind farm in Britain. The parts were made in Denmark.
	We took a picnic lunch on a 3 mile walk around the promontory NE of Hayle Bay. It was a glorious afternoon. Polzeath. Sunshine, majestic waves. Surfing. On to camp at Trelowarren Manor, St. Columbo.

swimming pool, games room for children. Nice and empty. V. clean loos.
   We walked into St. Columb. Nice old has-been town. Beautiful Georgian library [?] windows. We had a good beer at the pub, + fish + chips next door. Publican had brought back a 30 ft. R.V. from America.
May 3rd Tuesday
   We had a 50p bath at the campsite + left.
Rain.
   At __Truro__ the rain stopped leaving a blustery day. We visited the cathedral, mock Gothic, Bath stone & granite, 1910, the first to be finished in England since St. Paul's. Lovely organ music. Interesting monuments. We had elevenses in the Terrace & bought lunch at 17-S. Afterwards we walked up to look at the Law Courts 1986-88. Splendid modern architecture, circles, domes, a grass rectangle in front with skeletal square shapes to define it architecturally - brick, slate, thick glass. Strict colour schemes - white, grevy & green plants. Superb view of cathedral + distant pines + other trees on far hills.
   We drove on to __St. Ives__ & camped at Ayr * * * high on the hill, looking down onto the enormous cemeteries, the new art Gallery St. Ives Tate; pretty beach & gorgeous turquoise sea with white crests.

captions: Madonna + Child by Barbara Hepworth  St. Ives parish church  In memory of her son, Simon, killed in Thailand RAF 1953
pages.text:
Madonna + Child
   by
   Barbara Hepworth

St. Ives parish church

In memory of her son,
Simon, killed in
Thailand RAF
1953

The gallery shuts at 9.pm. We looked in. I like the arbs architecture, but not many of the exhibits. Alfred Wallis, an old sailor had some paintings there, & there was an alcove of exhibits showing friendship between artists.

We drank beer John Smith at the "Sloop on the harbour, and lovely Shellfish, brill & salmon at the "Mermaid". One beach out to the "Island" (with the little church with 2 crosses on) is calm & serene, uniform architecture & Tate, & the other has the fishing moke & tourist shops. The Tate was built in place of an old gasometer, enormous gas cylinder.
   May 4th Wednesday
      We woke to rain, but after 2 showers, the rest of the day has been sunny. We walked 20 minutes down the hill again to St. Ives church. It is very pretty, high, user-friendly with things explained as you walk round in the guide notes. The baptistary floor is paved [?≡III] by slates on end. Lovely alabaster, well lit behind the high altar, gilded saints in the wooden roof & a beautiful Madonna & child by Hepworth. The notes invited you to get your children to stroke the baby's head to feel how smooth the marble is.
   St. Ives houses were divided into "[?Upalong for] the tin miners & "Downalong" for the fishermen families. "Downalong" got all its water from

St. Ia's well. She floated to Cornwall on a leaf from Ireland.
   We bought a painting "Skin Deep" by Stephen Dove. We saw Barbara Hepworth's museum & very pretty garden. The Cornish Ice Cream is delicious. We bought shells for the boys & fudge, clotted cream fudge.
   We had lunch down a track on the way to St. Just. We also booked a trip for tomorrow down a tin mine.
   St. Just has an old, grass-covered circle/ amphitheatre where Mystery Plays were performed until the [?17], & a Methodist Church with an iron on the roof & Doric columns.
   The Anglican church has enormous stones in the walls, & wall paintings & a standing head stone from the [?5] [?with] Sedus Hic [?acest] on it. We had beer & mead liqueur in the pub. ****
   Our evening campsite is called Bosavern. We are camped in a beautiful lawned garden.
   May 5th
   I had a shower in the shower block. The garden is beautiful. They don't allow tents.
   We set off to look at a [?] BC grave [?through] pea soup fog. I was frightened of falling

of the edge of the world. We couldn't find it. We dropped out of the cloud down to Newlyn where the art gallery is being refurbished for another week, & on into __Penzance__ where we finally found a gallery, had a bad cup of coffee & bought cakes at a frigid, Snooty cake-shop. Lid words— def a no-no.

[glued-in newspaper/printed clipping:]
__THE CORNISH LANGUAGE__

[Marginalia to right of clipping, with small sketches of a curved bucket/bowl shape and a cart:]
Richard Trevithick
designed a bucket
for mining this
shape, [sketch of curved bucket shape] because
the tunnels were
this shape [sketch of oval/lozenge shape]
in the mines - it
cost less to mine
away less granite
at foot level.

[sketch of cart with wheels]
cart with
wheels, never
one end to go outside
earth.

We arrived at the Geevor tin mine & went underground at Zennot. We were taken round by 2 retired tin miners. 3 hours. It was fascinat[ing]. When one of them started in 1960, he was paid [by] the square fathom. Out of this money he had to pay to have his drills sharpened. The original hats were varnished felt. Until 1980s there was little awareness of safety, esp. in ventilation. 2 men banged a drill in - one held the drill, one turned it - 8 turns to a circle, until 1880s. Then compressed air drills came in, but men breathed the dust until water was passed along the central drill 1930s. Men stood on old fan belts looped over the handles once the drill was in one foot vertically. Originally, 1690, gunpowder was used with goosefeather quills, then dynamite 1880s + safety fuses 90 seconds per yard. These are still used where there might a lightening. Only too [more?] actually made the tin. At one time in 1930s they reached too near the sea in the Levant mine & a fissure opened which they plugged, (Shipconcrete 1960s) + carried on working underneath. Arsenic smells of garlic.

Afterwards we went to lands End a disastrous excursion where we were charged £6 for looking at the sea.
	We raced [struck: buy] back to St. Ives by 5.50. to buy a Mary Frost pastel for me "Solitude", to be turned out of the gallery because it had closed at 5. & they were having a meeting. Then they invited us back in! The man who told us to leave was Stephen Dove! Simon recognised him. He was embarrassed. ****
	We carried on to Mullions Cove, Griga Mill. One of the prettiest places we've seen, with nice looking chalets. The mill race runs beside the campsite.
	May 6th Friday
	We strolled out to look at the mole & rocks, where there were nesting sea gulls with the males standing sentinel.
	We left to go down the __Lizard__ further. We parked & had a 3½ hour walk around the Point. It was threatening rain but we stayed dry. I had a cream tea for elevenses. We passed the lighthouse, where there has been a light since 1752.
	There is beautiful lush vegetation along the path & hanging off the cliffs. 4 types of yellow flower

including lichen on the S. facing slopes, 2 blue, 2 [?st] white & pink campion. Even the dry stone walls are green & peoples' boots have polished the green lizard rocks in parts.
	We bought vases & pots out of green lizard serpentine rock. The red is more expensive. The rocks are becoming scarce. There are only 5 turners left.
	We bought delicious Ann's Pasties for lunch.
	We had tea in a layby & went to __Trebarh Gardens__. It was spectacular - designed in 1826 by [struck: James] Charles Fox, who stood up at the house with a telescope while his servants erected enormous scaffolding, so that he could anticipate what the effects of the trees would be when they were fully grown. 100 years being cared for, 40 years left, 10 years being cared for fiercely again. The trees are beautiful in a ravine valley, the tallest palms in England, Rhododendrons grown from seed in 1890, very tall now, & mega Brazilian rhubarb. A beach for picnic & swimming at the bottom. Fine sailing in Helston estuary. Reminiscient nostalgic smells from azaleas playing hide-and-seek in the garden.

pages.sketch_desc: Detailed pen sketch of the Royal Albert Bridge (Brunel's bridge) over the River Tamar at Saltash, viewed from ground level showing the bridge's distinctive spans and piers. Labels include 'TAMAR BRIDGE 1961', 'I.K. BRUNEL ENG. 1859', and 'R. Tamar' at bottom right. 'Cornwall' marked at bottom left and 'Devon' in the right margin, indicating the two counties the bridge connects.
pages.text:
We drove east + ended up having a good supper at the Stag at Liskeard before camping at Trethen Meanor, Liskeard. *** Sad, [has] been playground.

[Hand-drawn sketch of Royal Albert Bridge / Brunel's bridge over the River Tamar at Saltash, with labels:]
TAMAR BRIDGE 1961
I.K. BRUNEL ENG. 1859
R. Tamar

[Bottom left margin:] Cornwall
[Right margin, vertical:] Devon
[Left margin, vertical, near bottom:] Saltash
Exciting to see the Tamar
[Right margin, vertical:] Brunel's bridge, lots not written.

captions: The Higgledy-Piggledy Tree / The Rain Forest
pages.text:
[Left margin, vertical text, partially legible:] Saltash
Exciting to see the Tamar
Brunel's bridge, lots not written.

[Page contains six colour photographs, arranged in a 2×3 grid, with printed captions:]
[Top left photo caption:] Helford Vista
Trebah Valley
[Top right photo caption:] The Higgledy-Piggledy Tree
The Rain Forest
[Middle left photo caption:] [illegible/no caption visible]
[Middle right photo caption:] [illegible/no caption visible]
[Bottom left photo caption:] 100 Year Old Tree Fern
[Bottom right photo caption:] Tarzan's Camp

Saturday May 7
   Drove to Saltash Bridge. We saw a Train. Sunshine.
   We stopped for lunch at a wooden mill which had a museum, [?Ofculme], but we didn't see the museum because we had to go round with a bus party. Pretty looking. We could go another time. (Before lunch) we stopped to shop & visit for Chud at Dartington Cider Press) On to home up the I75.

Friday May 27th
   Simon, Eve, William, Peter, John, Charles, Justin, Buttor & Chris, au pair set off at 3.45. for Poltern to pick up Thomas.
   At 7.20. we try a new Welsh campsite __Blaensan__ [?annotated]. Well-sheltered by trees. A Victorian farmhouse with [?loo] for campers. The farmer's wife's daughter lives in a house just below the farm. Clean loo, friendly people.

Sat Simon, Eve, Thomas, Peter, John, Charles, walk 1½ miles to the Mountain Centre. It's very cold & damp. Chris & Eve were cold in the night. Eve completely lost her voice. Simon beamed a great smile at breakfast & commented "it will be a very restful weekend"
   The Mountain Centre was warm. Thomas, Peter & John went out with shrimp nets to pools & looked at what they found under microscopes. William & Justin stayed at the camp. Eve collected

[?topshit], helped by Charles, for her allotment, on the way back to the van.
   We had lunch. At the end of lunch Tim, Margaret & Helen arrived. Some sun.
   2.30. Simon, Thomas, Peter, Justin, Tim, Margaret & Helen went off on a 7 mile walk. William took John & Charles to look at the car dumps, over the stream in the wood. Chris & Eve rested & read & wrote letters. We saw a sheep being sheared on the farm.
   When the walking party returned at 6.30. we had a barbeque. It was a lovely evening with some sunshine.

   Sun A better day, sun at breakfast, but showers forecast for the afternoon. We all piled into the van & drove over a steep pass to [?Storod][?Silte]. The church service was in the evening. So we set off on a walk, instead. The others set off & by the time Simon & Eve left the van (notice about car burglaries) everyone had disappeared. So we followed Simon's map along the road & footpath 1½ miles to caves. __Porth-yr-Ogof__. Simon & Eve saw the cave & came up & the others arrived. We saw the cave again & potholers jumping off rocks & going into & out of the cave with crash helmets & lights. There is also a bench in proud & loving memory of Paul 1949-71 who in the cave sought truth & beauty.
   We went on to see the Blue Pool, where the
